_source: Kurczak & Fish, Art & Science of Vedic Astrology Vol II, Appendix III (p364-366) — 'Astrological Indications of Planets in Specific Houses Within the First Divisional Chart (the Birth Chart)'
_note: Deterministic reference: the indication of each planet when placed in each natal house. Feed to the narrative as concrete specifics; do NOT paraphrase into 'work'. COMPLETE, houses 1-12.

1.Mars: Character, Sense of Right and Wrong

2.Saturn: Thriftiness, Wealth from Hard Work, Rock Quarries

3.Sun: Intelligent Use and Consistency of Skills

5.Moon: Imagination, Ability to Improve One's Consciousness
5.Jupiter: Children, Father's Grandfather, Knowledge and Learning

6.Mercury: Maternal Aunts and Uncles, Legal Matters

7.Sun: Egotism of Spouse or Partner, Partner's Consistency
7.Saturn: Appreciation of Relationships, Lack of Empathy

8.Mercury: Corporations, Occult Knowledge, Past Life Fortune
8.Saturn: Chronic Disease, Death, Flaws, Weaknesses, Liabilities

9.Mars: Brother and Sister in Law, Moral Code
9.Mercury: Past Life Fortune, Judges, Codes of Conduct
9.Jupiter: Guru, Teacher, Ease of Fortune, Grand Children
9.Saturn: Delays to Fortune, Delays to Creativity

10.Sun: Government, Activity, Status, Power, Father's Influence

11.Mars: Character and Motivation of Group Dynamics

12.Mars: Results of Enemies Actions, Secret Enemies
12.Mercury: Long Term Investments, Contemplative Knowledge, Purchasing Expenses, Gain of Lost Items
12.Venus: Nursing Homes, Mistress, Mother's Father, Vacations
12.Saturn: Personal Expense, Loss, Capital Expense, Prisons, Imprisonment
